mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that remains stable or drifts lower over the trailing three months signals an accommodative policy stance that lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is always active and does not require a specific triggering event.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX returns following flat/declining FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than returns following rising-rate periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing three-month window, it signals a deteriorating labor market that leads investors to price in weaker corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based consumer and residential investment strength that jointly reinforces confidence in aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone captures this compounding demand signal; only their joint acceleration is predictive.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST acceleration signal produces forward 20-day SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the interaction term fails to add predictive power over either series individually in an ablation test (Rank IC improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, sustained industrial production (INDPRO) growth above trend signals capacity utilization pressures that foreshadow margin compression and slower forward earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. This effect is regime-specific and does not hold in early-cycle recoveries where INDPRO growth reflects genuine slack absorption.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth > 3% while in late-cycle regime
failure_condition: During identified late-cycle periods, forward 60-day SPX returns following high INDPRO growth are not significantly negative (p > 0.10), or the sign of the relationship reverses in more than 50% of late-cycle sub-periods tested.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) growth acceleration reflects improving labor income and consumption capacity, which is incorporated quickly into equity prices over a short one-week horizon, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This is an always-on momentum-style signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month PAYEMS growth and forward 5-day SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign flips in more than 40% of rolling 6-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) growth simultaneously decelerates, real policy tightness increases sharply, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither the level of FEDFUNDS nor CPIAUCSL alone is sufficient; the joint divergence between nominal rate direction and inflation trend drives the effect.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0 AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth decelerates > 0.2pp
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns under the joint trigger condition are not significantly more negative than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the interaction effect does not outperform single-series FEDFUNDS or CPIAUCSL signals in an ablation test.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% over a trailing six-month period signals weakening residential investment demand, a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns following a >10% six-month HOUST decline are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) growth and payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth simultaneously accelerate over a one-month window, it signals synchronized supply-side and labor-market strength that is rapidly priced into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal requires joint acceleration; either series alone is a weaker and less reliable predictor.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0.3% AND PAYEMS MoM growth > 0.15%
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO/PAYEMS acceleration signal produces forward 5-day SPX returns that are not significantly different from the unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or the joint signal underperforms either single-series signal in a walk-forward ablation test.
